close_reason: Set local_models.mode: shadow in routing.yaml. Commit 00c285a in Clavain repo.
description: Set local_models.mode: shadow in os/Clavain/config/routing.yaml so Clavain logs which tasks would route to local inference without actually routing them. This is the safe first step before enforce mode.
title: [interfere] Enable Track B5 shadow mode in Clavain routing.yaml

description: Clavain's 4-track routing is more intelligent than LiteLLM (task-aware complexity, evidence calibration, safety floors) but lacks operational capabilities: (1) cost tracking/attribution, (2) cloud provider fallback with retries, (3) rate limit management, (4) unified API gateway, (5) usage observability, (6) privacy-aware routing. With interfere providing local serving, Clavain needs these to route between local and cloud without a proxy.
title: Competitive Landscape: Close Clavain routing gaps vs LiteLLM/OpenRouter

description: Apple's M5 Max has a 16-core ANE that sits idle during MLX GPU inference. Research whether standard attention (Q@K^T → softmax → scores@V) can be offloaded to ANE while the GPU handles expert forward passes. This would break the serial GPU→SSD→GPU pipeline constraint that flash-moe identified. Risk: ANE programming is undocumented beyond CoreML. Approach: profile ANE utilization during inference, test CoreML attention dispatch.
title: [interfere] Experiment: ANE (Apple Neural Engine) offload for attention

description: Create interlab campaigns for each experiment: early exit (entropy-based layer skipping), reservoir routing (frozen-layer MLP), and any other planned techniques. Define baselines, success metrics, and kill conditions per AGENTS.md experiment specs.
title: [interfere] Start interlab experiment campaigns for esoteric techniques

description: Implement attention sink pattern (Xiao et al. 2023, arXiv 2309.17453) for long-running agent sessions. Keep KV states for initial tokens (attention sinks) + recent window only. Enables stable generation over 4M+ tokens without growing KV cache. 22x speedup over sliding window recompute. Critical for Clavain agents that run long sessions — currently context is bounded by KV cache size. mlx-lm already has --max-kv-size for a rotating cache; this extends it with attention sink preservation.
title: [interfere] Experiment: StreamingLLM attention sinks for infinite context

close_reason: GitHub repo created: https://github.com/mistakeknot/interfere (public). Initial commit with 38 files, 2905 lines. Branch protection: configure via dashboard.
description: Run gh repo create mistakeknot/interfere (public), push initial code, and add the repo to clone-subrepos.sh so it's included in Demarch ecosystem cloning.
title: [interfere] Create GitHub repo and add to clone-subrepos.sh

description: Run the downloaded Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit through interfere's server. Measure: tok/s, time-to-first-token, quality on standard prompts (coding, reasoning, tool calling). This is the smallest MoE in the Pareto frontier — 35B total, 3B active, ~20GB, should fit entirely in RAM.
title: [interfere] Benchmark Qwen3.5-35B-A3B 4-bit via mlx-lm

description: Implement per-token layer skipping via learned top-k routing (Raposo et al. 2024, arXiv 2404.02258). Each layer has a router that decides which tokens participate in self-attention and MLP vs skip. Expected: up to 50% faster inference at equivalent quality. Requires training a lightweight router per layer — could bootstrap from our reservoir_routing.py MLP approach. Composable with MoE: skip both experts AND layers for eligible tokens.
title: [interfere] Experiment: Mixture of Depths (MoD) dynamic layer routing

description: Google's TurboQuant (ICLR 2026, arxiv.org/abs/2504.19874) achieves 3-bit KV cache quantization with zero accuracy loss on long-context benchmarks. Two-stage approach: (1) PolarQuant converts KV to polar coordinates, maps to fixed circular grid (eliminates expensive normalization), (2) QJL adds 1-bit residual error correction via Johnson-Lindenstrauss Transform. Results: 6x KV memory reduction, 8x performance increase on H100 GPUs vs 32-bit. Tested on Gemma, Mistral, Llama-3.1-8B. This is strictly better than our planned Q8K/Q4V approach (Demarch-dw8) — 3-bit with ZERO quality loss vs 4-8 bit with potential degradation. For interfere: (1) implement PolarQuant for MLX/Metal, (2) measure KV savings on Qwen3.5-35B and 122B, (3) combine with StreamingLLM sinks for near-infinite context. Companion paper: PolarQuant (AISTATS 2026, arxiv.org/abs/2502.02617).
title: [interfere] Experiment: TurboQuant (PolarQuant+QJL) for 3-bit KV cache compression

description: Build flash-moe, extract weights from the downloaded Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-4bit, and benchmark. Measure: tok/s at 4-bit, expert cache hit rate, per-layer timing. Compare to flash-moe's M3 Max results (4.36 tok/s). Estimate: 7-10 tok/s on our M5 Max 128GB.
title: [interfere] Benchmark Qwen3.5-397B-A17B via flash-moe SSD streaming

description: When multiple Clavain agents send requests simultaneously, currently they queue sequentially (MLX constraint: no concurrent inference). Research: (1) continuous batching — accumulate requests into micro-batches for shared prefill, (2) sequence-level parallelism within a batch, (3) request priority with preemption (interrupt low-pri generation when high-pri arrives). Expected: better throughput for the common case of 3-5 concurrent subagents during flux-drive reviews.
title: [interfere] Experiment: adaptive batching for concurrent agent requests

close_reason: Feasibility study complete. M5 Max SSD benchmarks: 36 GB/s seq, 27 GB/s random pread (1.5-2x faster than M3 Max test rig). Estimated 7-10 tok/s for 397B model at 4-bit. Recommend subprocess bridge integration. Doc: docs/investigations/2026-03-26-flash-moe-feasibility.md
description: Research integrating flash-moe (github.com/danveloper/flash-moe) into the interfere pipeline. flash-moe is a pure C/Metal engine that runs Qwen3.5-397B-A17B by streaming expert weights from SSD. On a 48GB M3 Max it achieves 4.36 tok/s at 4-bit. On our 128GB M5 Max, expert caching would be dramatically better (most active experts stay in RAM), potentially 8-12+ tok/s. Integration options: (1) subprocess bridge calling flash-moe binary, (2) port SSD-streaming techniques into our MLX pipeline, (3) hybrid where flash-moe handles C3+ tasks and mlx-lm handles C1-C2. This would be a game-changer for sovereignty — running a 397B model locally. Should be an interlab experiment campaign.
title: [interfere] Investigate flash-moe integration for 397B MoE inference

close_reason: Server runnable via 'uv run python -m server [--dry-run]'. InferenceEngine verified with mlx-community/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct-4bit. 18/18 tests pass. Changes local only — no git repo yet (Demarch-26p).
description: Download a working model (qwen3:30b via ollama or equivalent MLX model) and verify it serves completions via the /v1/chat/completions endpoint. This validates the inference path end-to-end.
title: [interfere] Pull and serve a model (ollama or MLX)

description: Run the downloaded Qwen3.5-122B-A10B-4bit through interfere's server. Measure: tok/s, time-to-first-token, quality on standard prompts. 122B total, 10B active, ~65GB, fits in RAM on 128GB M5 Max.
title: [interfere] Benchmark Qwen3.5-122B-A10B 4-bit via mlx-lm

description: Once downloaded (~449GB), benchmark Kimi K2.5-3bit. 1T total params, 32B active, 384 experts K=8. Largest model in the frontier. Will require SSD streaming engine. Quality at 3-bit quantization needs verification — may need 4-bit if quality degrades.
title: [interfere] Benchmark Kimi K2.5 3-bit (1T, 32B active)

description: Clavain agents share substantial prompt prefixes (system prompts, CLAUDE.md context, skill injections). mlx-lm supports prompt caching. Implement: (1) hash-based prefix cache that persists across requests, (2) measure cache hit rate for typical Clavain workflows (brainstorm→plan→execute share ~80% prefix), (3) integrate with the priority queue to deduplicate shared prefixes. Expected: 40-60% TTFT reduction for second and subsequent agent requests in a sprint.
title: [interfere] Experiment: prompt caching across agent sessions

description: Once downloaded (~419GB), benchmark GLM-5-4bit. GLM-5 benchmarks competitively with Claude Opus 4.5 (77.8 SWE-bench, 61.1 Terminal-Bench). 744B total, 40B active, uses DeepSeek Sparse Attention. Will require SSD streaming engine. This could be the best local model for coding tasks.
title: [interfere] Benchmark GLM-5 4-bit (744B, 40B active)

description: Parent epic for interfere initial bringup. Enable shadow routing in Clavain, serve a model via ollama/MLX, wire Metal worker to InferenceEngine, start interlab experiment campaigns, and create the GitHub repo. These are the first concrete steps to make interfere operational.
title: [interfere] Phase 1: Bring up local inference pipeline

description: Use Qwen3.5-9B as a draft model to accelerate generation from larger models (122B, 397B). Speculative decoding generates candidate tokens with the fast model, then verifies in parallel with the target model. Expected: 2-2.5x speedup (per Leviathan et al. 2023). mlx-lm had a speculative_decoding example. Measure: tok/s improvement, rejection rate, quality parity.
title: [interfere] Experiment: speculative decoding with small draft model

description: Esoteric approach inspired by swarm intelligence. Track which expert sequences produce high-confidence outputs (pheromone trails). Over many requests, expert routing paths that frequently produce good results get reinforced, while poor paths decay. This creates an emergent 'highway system' through the expert space. Could dramatically improve page cache hit rates for SSD streaming — if the same expert sequences are preferred, they stay warm in cache. Research-grade, high risk/high reward.
title: [interfere] Experiment: ant colony optimization for expert routing paths

close_reason: Metal worker wired to InferenceEngine. Full pipeline: HTTP → Metal subprocess → mlx-lm stream_generate → SSE. Verified e2e with Qwen2.5-0.5B. Commit 188e350.
description: Connect the Metal subprocess worker (server/metal_worker.py) to actually call InferenceEngine (server/inference.py). Currently the queue-to-inference plumbing is incomplete — the worker receives requests but doesn't execute inference.
title: [interfere] Wire Metal worker to InferenceEngine

description: Quantize the Key-Value cache to reduce memory pressure. Keys to 8-bit, Values to 4-bit (asymmetric quantization since values are more tolerant). KIVI (2024) shows 2-bit KV is feasible. ThinK (2024) shows channel pruning in KV cache gives 20% reduction composable with quantization. On 128GB M5 Max this could: (1) fit larger KV context windows, (2) leave more RAM for page cache when SSD-streaming experts, (3) enable running 397B with larger batch sizes. The AGENTS.md already references Q8K/Q4V in the memory budget.
title: [interfere] Experiment: quantized KV cache (Q8K/Q4V)

description: Esoteric approach: treat expert selection as a simulated annealing problem. Instead of always picking top-K experts by router score, use a temperature parameter that starts high (more exploration of diverse experts) and anneals low (exploit best experts) as generation progresses. Hypothesis: early tokens benefit from diverse expert perspectives (like brainstorming), while later tokens benefit from focused expertise (like editing). Could compose with MoE streaming to predictively pre-fetch 'warm' experts.
title: [interfere] Experiment: thermodynamic-inspired annealing for expert selection

description: Extend the existing reservoir_routing.py experiment. Train a 262K-param MLP on frozen layer-24 hidden states to classify task type (coding, reasoning, creative, factual). Use classification to route to the optimal model before full generation starts. Current: skeleton exists in server/experiments/reservoir_routing.py. Need: (1) collect hidden state dataset from 200-500 examples per routing class, (2) train MLP, (3) integrate with confidence cascade for two-signal routing (task type + confidence).
title: [interfere] Experiment: reservoir computing readout for task routing

description: Port key optimizations from flash-moe's hand-written Metal shaders into our MLX pipeline: (1) FMA-optimized dequantization (+12% throughput), (2) fused SwiGLU activation (eliminates CPU roundtrip), (3) fused RoPE with Q deinterleave. flash-moe proved these beat generic MLX kernels. The question: can we get flash-moe-level per-kernel performance while keeping the mlx-lm Python framework for model flexibility? Start with the FMA dequant kernel as proof-of-concept.
title: [interfere] Experiment: custom Metal compute shaders for mlx-lm

description: DeepSeek-V2 introduced MLA which compresses KV states into a latent vector, achieving 93.3% KV cache reduction with 5.76x throughput increase. Research whether MLA can be applied as a post-training retrofit to existing models (Qwen3.5 uses standard GQA). If not retrofittable, MLA-native models (DeepSeek V3.2, Kimi K2.5) already have this — measure the KV savings on our hardware. This determines whether DeepSeek/Kimi have a structural advantage over Qwen for long-context local inference.
title: [interfere] Experiment: Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA) for KV compression

close_reason: Updated routing.yaml tier_mappings and complexity_routing to MoE-first models (Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, Nemotron-Cascade-2-30B-A3B, gpt-oss-120b). Updated AGENTS.md memory budget. Clavain commit 145a83b, interfere commit 0e9e57b.
description: Replace the current complexity-to-model mapping in routing.yaml and AGENTS.md with newer MoE models that are significantly faster and better quality. Current: qwen3-8b/qwen3-30b/qwen2.5-72b. Proposed: Qwen3.5-9B-OptiQ-4bit (C1 draft), Qwen3.5-35B-A3B 4-bit (C2, MoE 3B active), Nemotron-Cascade-2-30B-A3B 8-bit or gpt-oss-120b MXFP4 (C3). MoE models give big-model quality at small-model speed because only a fraction of params are active per token. Also update the memory budget in AGENTS.md to reflect actual model sizes.
title: [interfere] Update model tier routing to MoE-first models

description: After all benchmarks complete, compile results into a Pareto frontier analysis. Plot tok/s vs quality (SWE-bench proxy, coding eval, tool-calling success). Determine optimal routing: which model for which complexity tier. Update routing.yaml with empirically validated tiers. This is the capstone deliverable for the model evaluation campaign.
title: [interfere] Publish Pareto frontier analysis: speed vs quality across local models

description: Implement early exit with self-speculative verification (LayerSkip, arXiv 2404.16710). Early layers generate draft tokens, remaining layers verify. No separate draft model needed — uses the target model's own early layers. Expected: 1.8-2.2x speedup. This extends our existing early_exit.py hook from entropy-based exit to self-speculative verification. Key advantage: shares compute/activations between draft and verify, reducing memory vs standard speculative decoding.
title: [interfere] Experiment: LayerSkip / self-speculative early exit

description: Implement the confidence cascade described in routing.yaml: start inference with the smallest model, measure first-3-token average probability. If confidence > 0.8: accept. If 0.6-0.8: try next larger model. If < 0.6: escalate to cloud. This is the quality gate for local-vs-cloud routing — the key mechanism that makes sovereignty practical. Requires: (1) token-level probability extraction from mlx-lm, (2) fast model switching in the Metal worker, (3) latency budget tracking (cascade can't be slower than just calling the big model).
title: [interfere] Experiment: confidence cascade with multi-model waterfall

description: Once downloaded (~378GB), benchmark DeepSeek V3.2-4bit. Will require SSD streaming engine (flash-moe port or mlx-lm with offloading). 672B total, ~37B active. Measure tok/s and quality vs Qwen3.5 family.
title: [interfere] Benchmark DeepSeek V3.2 4-bit (672B, ~37B active)

description: When running 397B+ models via SSD streaming, the OS page cache is critical (~71% hit rate on flash-moe). Investigate: (1) mlx-lm's memory wiring feature (macOS 15+) — wire model weights to prevent page-out, (2) madvise() hints for expert access patterns, (3) pre-warming the page cache with predicted experts based on routing decisions from previous tokens, (4) measuring actual cache hit rate on M5 Max 128GB (should be much higher than M3 Max 48GB). Expected: 10-20% throughput improvement from cache optimization alone.
title: [interfere] Experiment: memory wiring and page cache optimization for SSD streaming
